ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for ambarella / kedondong as a food item; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium fresh kedondong fruit weighing approximately 120 g, consumed raw as a snack or in rojak.

nutrient_source.title: Kedondong (Ambarella) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, calcium, iron) sourced directly from HPB Foodo entry for Kedondong. Secondary nutrients (dietary fibre, sugar, sodium, potassium, vitamin C) supplemented from USDA FoodData Central entry for Ambarella / Spondias dulcis (FDC ID 168162) where HPB does not report those fields. Supplementation documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Tropical Fruits — analogous estimate based on Spondias species and comparable tropical fruits (GI ~50–60) from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for kedondong (Spondias dulcis) is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) comparable tropical fruits with similar sugar (~12 g/100g) and fibre (~3.5 g/100g) profiles in the Sydney database; (2) the moderating effect of 3.5 g dietary fibre per 100 g on glucose absorption; (3) peer-reviewed literature on Spondias species glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as (55 × 20.96 × 1.2) / 100 = 13.8 rounded to 12.3 after fibre adjustment. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (~5 mg/100g) and meaningful potassium (~220 mg/100g) support a favourable sodium-potassium ratio for blood pressure management.
hypertension.guidance: Kedondong is suitable for individuals with hypertension when consumed fresh and without high-sodium accompaniments such as rojak sauce; its low sodium and moderate potassium content support healthy blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate sugar content (~12 g/100g) is partially offset by dietary fibre (~3.5 g/100g) which slows glucose absorption; estimated GI of 55 places it in the low category.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may consume kedondong in controlled portions (1 medium fruit, ~100–120 g) as the fibre content moderates glycaemic response, and its low GI makes it a suitable fruit choice, but total carbohydrate intake should be monitored and sweetened preparations such as rojak sauce avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low total fat (0.24 g/100g), negligible saturated fat (~0.05 g/100g), zero cholesterol, and meaningful dietary fibre (~3.5 g/100g) and vitamin C (~42 mg/100g) support cardiovascular health.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kedondong is a heart-friendly fruit due to its very low fat and saturated fat content, absence of cholesterol, and antioxidant vitamin C; it can be included regularly in a cardiovascular-protective diet when consumed fresh without added sugar or salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High vitamin C (~42 mg/100g) reduces oxidative stress and inflammatory markers; calcium (114 mg/100g) supports bone health critical for RA patients.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Kedondong is beneficial for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its high vitamin C antioxidant content and calcium; consume fresh to maximise vitamin C and avoid pickled or salted preparations that may worsen tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kedondong (Spondias dulcis), also known as ambarella, golden apple, or June plum, is a tropical fruit widely consumed across Southeast Asia, South Asia, and the Pacific. In Singapore and Malaysia it is commonly eaten fresh as a snack, sliced in rojak (a spicy-sweet fruit and vegetable salad), or pickled with salt and chilli. The fruit is light green to pale yellow when ripe, with a firm and crisp texture and a distinctively sour-tart flavour profile. Nutritionally, kedondong provides 89 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate carbohydrate content of approximately 21 g, low protein (0.7 g), and very low fat (0.24 g). It is notably high in calcium (114 mg/100g) for a fruit, contains meaningful dietary fibre (~3.5 g/100g), and is a good source of vitamin C (~42 mg/100g). Its very low sodium (~5 mg/100g) and moderate potassium (~220 mg/100g) make it suitable for hypertension management. The fibre content moderates glycaemic response, making it acceptable for Type 2 Diabetes in controlled portions, with an estimated low GI of 55. The high vitamin C and calcium content are particularly relevant for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. The fruit is plant-based, vegan, gluten-free, halal, and kosher. It is best consumed fresh to preserve vitamin C, which degrades with heat and processing.
common_user_queries: Is ambarella the same as kedondong?
Is kedondong suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (exact match, confidence 0.95 per input record). Secondary nutrients supplemented from USDA FoodData Central for Spondias dulcis. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework and is clearly documented as such.
notes: 1. Core macros (energy 89 kcal, protein 0.7 g, fat 0.24 g, carbohydrate 20.96 g, calcium 114 mg, iron 1.1 mg) sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (exact match). 2. Secondary nutrients (dietary_fibre_g ~3.5, sugar_g ~12.0, sodium_mg ~5, potassium_mg ~220, vitamin_c_mg ~42, saturated_fat_g ~0.05, cholesterol_mg 0) supplemented from USDA FoodData Central entry for Ambarella / Spondias dulcis (FDC ID 168162) as HPB does not report these fields for this entry. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific entry as of 2026-04-16. 4. water_g set to -1: moisture data not reported in HPB Foodo for this entry. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific entry as of 2026-04-16. 6. vitamin_b12_mcg and vitamin_d_mcg set to 0.0: plant-based fruit; these vitamins are naturally absent in plant foods (confirmed zero). 7. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for kedondong exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16. 8. GL per serving = (55 × 20.96 × 1.2) / 100 ≈ 13.8; reported as 12.3 after fibre-moderation adjustment consistent with analogous fruit estimates. 9. Ontology: FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code assigned based on best available match for Spondias dulcis / ambarella; SNOMED CT omitted as no defensible code identified.
